name: Old Fashioned Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup cold butter, cut into small pieces
3/4 cup sugar, plus 1 1/3 tablespoons sugar, divided
8 large Granny Smith apples (about 4 pounds), halved, cored and peeled, then cut into 1/2 inch chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, bring the cider to a boil and reduce to about 1/2 cup. Cool for about 20 minutes.
2. Crust: Mix together cider and water. In a food processor, mix flour and butter until coarse crumbs form. Pulse on and off until dough forms. Add the cider-water mixture with the motor running until dough is just coming out of the bowl. Form into two balls. Divide each ball in half and flatten into disks. Wrap in plastic wrap and chill for 30-45 minutes.
3. Filling: In a bowl, mix together sugar, flour, cinnamon, and nutmeg. Add apples to sugar mixture and toss to coat well. Sprinkle remaining apple cider over all and toss to coat well.
4. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). On a lightly floured surface, roll out half of dough to a 12-inch circle. Place in a 9-inch pie plate. Spoon apple mixture into pie shell, mounding high in the center. Roll remaining dough into a 12-inch circle and place over filling.
5. Press edges together and roll up together to form an even rim. Decoratively flute the edge.
6. Brush top of pastry with water and sprinkle evenly with 1 1/2 tablespoons sugar. Cut slits in the top of the pie for steam to escape.
7. Place pie on a baking sheet and bake at 425°F (220°C) for 30 minutes. Reduce temperature to 350°F (175°C) and continue baking for 45 more minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and apples are bubbly. If the top crust browns too quickly, loosely cover with foil.
8. Cool on a wire rack. Serve warm.
